title: Cochlioda sanguinea
taxonomy: Cochlioda sanguinea is a species of orchid, which is classified within the family Orchidaceae. This family is further divided into several subfamilies, including the Epidendroideae subfamily, which Cochlioda sanguinea is a part of. The genus Cochlioda also belongs to the tribe Maxillarieae. Cochlioda sanguinea is native to South America and is known for its vibrant flowers. As a member of the Orchidaceae family, it is also known as a monocot.

intro: Cochlioda sanguinea, also known as the red orchid, is a species of terrestrial orchid native to South America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Cochlioda sanguinea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
